name: Peanut Butter Cup Cheesecake from Kaitlin Young
recipeIngredient: 6 1.8 oz packages (2 per package) chopped peanut butter cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. In a food processor, grind cookies into fine crumbs. Add melted butter and process until well mixed. Press crumb mixture into bottom and two-thirds up the sides of a 9-inch spring-form pan. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat together cream cheese and sugar with an electric mixer on medium speed until light, fluffy, and smooth (2-3 minutes). Beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add flour, milk, and vanilla; beat until well blended and smooth, about 4 minutes.
3. Sprinkle the packages of chopped peanut butter cups evenly over the bottom of the chocolate crust. Carefully pour cheesecake mixture over all; spread evenly.
4. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 250°F and bake an additional 40-50 minutes, or until edges are set and cake jiggles only slightly in the center.
5. Let cheesecake cool at room temperature, then refrigerate until well chilled, about 6 hours or overnight. Run a knife around the pan edge to loosen the cake, and remove. Shortly before serving, garnish with whipped cream and sprinkle two remaining packages of chopped peanut butter cups on top.
